concepts.title: Lie groups and matrix examples
concepts.blurb: Lie groups combine smooth manifolds with group multiplication.

concepts.title: Lie algebra as tangent space at the identity
concepts.blurb: The Lie algebra $\mathfrak{g} = T_e G$ linearizes a Lie group at the identity; for matrix groups it is cut out by differentiating the defining equation along $A(0)=I$.

concepts.title: Lie algebra and exponential map
concepts.blurb: The exponential map $\exp:\mathfrak{g}\to G$ integrates each $X\in\mathfrak g$ to the one-parameter subgroup $t\mapsto\exp(tX)$, turning infinitesimal data into group elements.

concepts.title: Bracket, commutators, and BCH
concepts.blurb: The commutator $[X,Y]=XY-YX$ measures how $\exp(X)$ and $\exp(Y)$ fail to commute; Baker–Campbell–Hausdorff reconstructs the group product as an iterated-bracket series.

concepts.title: SO(3), SU(2), and the double cover
concepts.blurb: The simply-connected $\mathrm{SU}(2)=S^3$ is the universal cover of $\mathrm{SO}(3)=\mathbb{RP}^3$ with kernel $\{\pm I\}$ — the spinor sign that distinguishes fermions from bosons.

concepts.title: Adjoint representation and Killing form
concepts.blurb: Conjugation $\mathrm{Ad}_g(X)=gXg^{-1}$ gives the adjoint rep; its differential recovers the bracket, and the Killing form $B(X,Y)=\tr(\ad_X\ad_Y)$ detects semisimplicity.

concepts.title: Adjoint action and root data
concepts.blurb: Adjoint representations and root systems classify semisimple behavior.
